name: Garlic
description: Garlic (Allium sativum) is one of the most toxic foods for cats, significantly more dangerous than onion — roughly 3 to 5 times more potent by weight. It contains organosulfur compounds including N-propyl disulfide and allicin derivatives that cats cannot safely metabolise. These compounds oxidise haemoglobin inside red blood cells, forming Heinz bodies and triggering haemolytic anaemia.
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: N-propyl disulfide, allicin, and allyl disulfides; all forms are dangerous — raw, cooked, dried, or powdered
Mechanism: oxidative damage to haemoglobin creates Heinz bodies; the spleen destroys affected RBCs faster than they can be replaced, causing haemolytic anaemia
Symptoms: lethargy, weakness, pale or yellowish gums, rapid breathing, reduced appetite, reddish-brown urine; onset is often delayed 3–5 days after ingestion
Garlic powder is especially dangerous — concentrated and easily hidden in human food
Never share garlic bread, sauces, broth, or any seasoned food with cats; contact your vet immediately if ingestion is suspected

symptoms.name: Because garlic is a member of the Allium family and contains potent organosulfur compounds, it causes symptoms very similar to onion toxicosis
Vomiting, Diarrhea, Abdominal Pain, and Drooling
symptoms.onset: Often within 24 hours, though it can be delayed 2 to 4 days
symptoms.notes: These are typically the first signs of toxicosis to appear2. Unaged garlic preparations contain specific compounds that can cause direct, painful damage to the gastric and ileal mucosa, resulting in severe abdominal pain and diarrhea45. The toxins can also cause direct irritation to the mouth, leadi

symptoms.name: Lethargy, Weakness, and Depression
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days (typically 2 to 4 days)
symptoms.notes: These symptoms emerge as a secondary response to systemic hypoxia caused by the profound destruction of oxygen-carrying red blood cells (hemolytic anemia)

symptoms.name: Pale Mucous Membranes and Jaundice (Icterus)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days
symptoms.notes: Pale gums indicate a lack of oxygenated blood (anemia), while jaundice (yellowing of the skin/mucosa) indicates severe red blood cell destruction leading to an excess of bilirubin in the blood (hyperbilirubinemia)

symptoms.name: Tachypnea and Dyspnea (Breathing Difficulties)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days
symptoms.notes: This is a compensatory response to the decreased blood oxygen transportation capacity and resulting tissue hypoxia

symptoms.name: Tachycardia and Hypotension (Cardiovascular Distress)
symptoms.notes: Tachycardia develops as the heart pumps faster to compensate for impaired oxygen transportation6. Furthermore, garlic contains pharmacologically active agents (allicin and ajoene) that act as potent cardiac and smooth muscle relaxants, vasodilators, and hypotensive agents4. These hypotensive propert

symptoms.name: Hemoglobinuria (Dark Urine)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days
symptoms.notes: This is a hallmark sign of severe oxidative hemolysis, where damaged red blood cells rupture and release hemoglobin, which is then excreted through the urinary tract

symptoms.name: Cold Sensitivity
symptoms.notes: Affected cats may have difficulty regulating their body temperature due to the systemic effects of the anemia and hypoxia

symptoms.name: Garlic Breath
symptoms.notes: While not dangerous itself, this symptom is specifically associated with recent ingestion and serves as a highly valuable diagnostic clue for veterinarians
